nodes.title: We ruled out using the claims table for retrieval in Spec 1, opting instead to use evidence records.
We accepted using source_date and freshness_state on evidence_records for trust and freshness metrics.
We decided to use Phi-4 14B over Qwen3 8B due to better concept coverage and reasoning depth.
We chose v2 prompt over multi-pass due to its simpler architecture and ability to produce inferred claims.
We ruled out LoRA because a post-processor can handle formatting and the prompt addresses inference needs.
The team accepted the risk of marking newer claims as superseded to remove duplicate Phi-4 claims with >0.99 similarity from the same source page.
The team decided to keep both Phi-4 claims when they have a cosine similarity between 0.95 and 0.99 from the same source page because they are semantically close but distinct.
The team decided not to use LoRA fine-tuning because Phi-4 14B already outputs valid JSON and other issues were addressed by redesigning the prompt.
The team chose not to use LoRA fine-tuning due to the risk of creating a model tied to a specific base version, requiring re-training on every Phi-4 update.
The team deferred LoRA indefinitely because there were no remaining problems for it to solve after addressing other issues.
The team decided to run claims extraction locally using open-weight models via Ollama to eliminate per-token API costs.
The team chose to maintain embeddings as API calls due to their low cost with text-embedding-3-small.
The extraction pipeline was split into two paths: API extraction using Sonnet for high quality at a cost, and local extraction with open-weight models for ~80-90% quality without charge.
We chose local extraction over API extraction because it allows processing the full corpus in 2-3 hours on M4 Pro hardware.
We are testing llama3.1:8b model for local extraction because it has already been installed and passed quality tests.
We are downloading qwen3:8b model for testing due to its expected stronger JSON compliance.
We decided to replace the Supabase JS Client with the direct pg driver for database interactions.
We decided to change getEvaluationWithMatches in lib/storage.ts to accept lookup by test_id.
We chose Sonnet 4.5 over Haiku for better layer tagging and confidence calibration on ambiguous source material.
We added a stance mapping function to align the seed JSON's vocabulary with the DB enum.
We decided to use the Wipe + Re-Seed approach instead of the backfill script for fixing orphaned claims.
We ruled out using large INSERT statements without batching due to context/timeout limits.
The team chose a full wipe and re-seeding over backfill-matching due to unrecoverable provenance and unreliable linkage.
The team used static JSON linkage instead of runtime evidence lookup to avoid DB round-trips.
The team decided to build a production-grade functional clone of WorkDirectory.ca without reusing legacy code.
The team chose Next.js + TypeScript + Postgres + Redis + object storage + background workers for the MVP rebuild of WorkDirectory.ca.
The team accepted a parallel run with shadow traffic checks, metric parity checks, staged release by surface, and rollback switch for the cutover of WorkDirectory.ca.
The team decided to keep the post-processor for local models.
The team chose Sonnet 4.5 over Haiku for better layer tagging, hedging preservation, and confidence calibration.
The team applied a NOT NULL constraint to the source_id column in the claims table.
The team rewrote the seed route to load SEED_DATA from a JSON file with source IDs.
The team chose static JSON linkage over runtime evidence record lookup for seed route data loading.
We decided to fix the cosine distance gate in Assay from 0.10 to 0.05.
We decided to remove 'Claude is thinking' and replace it with 'Analyzing proposal...' on the Eval Progress Overlay.
We chose to remove 'Submitted' status from the UI, making proposals transition directly from draft to evaluated.
We decided to delete old demo products (Dashboard Core, Mobile App, API Platform) from the database.
We redesigned the citation display to show source name, relevance reason, and link.
We decided to set all layer weights to 1.0/1.0/1.0 for both test_eval and strategic modes.
We decided to remove graph visualization from the primary experience because it does not serve the core intent.
We decided to replace the evaluation pipeline in mcp-server/src/index.ts with a single evaluateProposal() call from shared lib.
We chose ESM script over API route because it avoids HTTP and is simpler to run.
We chose to test eval only for LLM calls because strategic cases have no expected verdict and skipping saves cost.
We decided not to ship Batch Evaluation because it has lower impact compared to other features identified for making the product showable.
We decided to send the repository to Lucas because it is a high-leverage action that costs nothing and was offered by him.
We decided not to port the full REST API for web UI from the prototype to the public assay repo because Assay is MCP-only and has no web UI.
We chose to replace the Supabase JS client with direct pg Pool in lib/db.ts because it was deprecated and kept only for client-side code.
We're sunsetting the Vercel cron job for Notion sync because it was replaced by SyncManager (PRD 18/20).
We decided to ship the 3-mode extraction routing for users needing free/paid/subscription options.
We decided to include a health_check MCP tool due to the absence of a web UI requiring a diagnostic tool.
We chose plainto_tsquery('english', search_query) over to_tsquery('english', search_query) because it prevents crashes on short/multi-word queries.
We decided to replace date-based grouping with Notion page name grouping for better organization.
We decided to use Relational DB as the system of record instead of Vector DB.
The team decided to add source_type and source_external_id columns to evidence_records to enable efficient querying by source type.
The team decided to implement tombstoning by adding is_tombstoned and tombstone_reason columns to preserve historical data while excluding it from retrieval.
We decided to ship the tombstoned records feature for better data retrieval with warning flags.
We will use nullable V3 schema columns now, even though the prompt isn't final.
We decided to rename 'Deep signal' to 'Subsurface insight' across all files.
We chose to enrich evidence payloads with inferAuthority, inferCustomerVsInternal, and batchFetchClaims functions.
We will use the Anthropic SDK no longer in briefingCore.ts.
The team decided to consolidate MCP tools from nine to three for efficiency.
The team decided to change all console.log statements to console.error in lib/syncManager.ts to prevent MCP JSON-RPC transport corruption.
The team decided to wire the briefing model to DB config by reading retrieval_config.briefing_model instead of using a hardcoded value.
We decided to disable the Anthropic API on all web routes, using local Phi-4 extraction instead.
We chose the Phi-4 model for local extraction after testing eight models, optimizing its prompt version two.
We decided to configure INTELLIGENCE_LEDGER_API_KEY in the environment for MCP server changes.
We decided to use two Node.js scripts that operate directly against the Notion API and local PostgreSQL.
We accepted the use of SHA-256 hashing per chunk to ensure deduplication in content processing.
The team modified processSourceClaims in lib/claims.ts to use supersede instead of delete.
The DELETE operation in app/api/evidence/reseed-corpus/route.ts was converted to a soft delete.
We decided to add a content hash check and duplicate handling in the POST /api/evidence (manual create) route.
We decided to skip inserting duplicate records in the POST /api/evidence/import-notion route and count them as 'unchanged'.
We decided to filter and process only new records in the POST /api/evidence/import-csv route by computing content hashes.
We decided to remove 'History' and 'Graph' from the sidebar because they led nowhere, damaging user trust.
We chose to remove jargon-heavy labels like '52 canonical / 0 deduped' and monospace uppercase field labels to better align with the user's mental model.
We decided to remove 'PROTOTYPE ENV' badge and 'System Status: All Systems Operational' footer from production as they signal that the product is not ready.
We decided to reduce the home page workspaces from seven surfaces to two: Quick Check and Recent Activity.
We ruled out stat cards and other panels like Top Recurring Conflicts from the home page because they are secondary information.
The team removed the PROTOTYPE ENV badge from the header to avoid framing every interaction as potentially unreliable.
We decided to remove History and Graph from the sidebar because they led to non-functional pages.
We decided to use hybrid search on evidence records instead of claim-based retrieval.
We will switch to claim-level retrieval by routing hybridEvidenceSearch() to the claims table instead of evidence_records when needed.
We decided to use local-first architecture for the closed beta to ensure zero compliance and data liability.
We chose to maintain one canonical private repo named intelligence-ledger-prototype as the single source of truth.
We're sunsetting the public repo named assay-mcp-server in favor of it becoming a frozen portfolio artifact.
We decided to delete accumulationLoop.ts because it was only a commented-out import.
We decided to rename 'check_proposal' to 'stress_test' for opt-in judgment mode.
We chose to structure output around PM judgment artifacts instead of generic summarization.
We decided to add a mode toggle on the evaluation page for 'Evaluate a test' and 'Ask a question'.
The team decided to replace the claims-primary / evidence-fallback chain with parallel retrieval in lib/evaluationCore.ts.
The team decided to save match results using evidence_record.id as the evidence_id instead of claim.id.
The team accepted that evidence records without content will be synthesized from associated claim texts as a safety net.
The team decided to fetch evidence_records from Supabase for updating the corpus-seed-data.json.
The team chose to match sections by title exact match as primary and by source_ref contains page_id AND title contains section_title as fallback.
The team decided to update content and summary of matched records, truncating the summary to the first 3 sentences.
We decided to consolidate six documents into one document summarizing V2's strengths, weaknesses, root causes, fixes, and shipping status.
We chose to eliminate two of the three evaluation layers for an internal test feature with no users.
We ruled out the sequential gate process involving eight PRDs for a product with no users as inefficient.
We decided to ship the product with v1 claims to one PM this week for actionable feedback.
We chose to kill the evaluation harness as a blocking dependency and make it a background process.
We will build the self-service onboarding flow to enable a second PM to set up independently.
The team decided to open the MCP server's distribution layer for self-service onboarding.
We decided to embed claims, not documents, because it is the highest-leverage architectural decision.
We chose OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for embeddings because it is the cheapest and most stable option available.
We will use workspace_id on all new tables to prevent a painful refactor later, even though the system is currently single-tenant.
We decided to make step number text-lg and inline with the title for visual hierarchy.
We decided to replace 'BM25' with 'full-text search' for accuracy in technical documentation.
We decided to add bold subheadings to problem card descriptions for better scannability.
The team decided to test health_check tool against a live instance to ensure it returns valid structured JSON.
We decided to note that EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=local is available using BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 via ONNX.
We decided to add a Glossary section with clear definitions of technical terms.
We decided to bold key terms like MCP server, PostgreSQL, pgvector, Claude Code, and reciprocal rank fusion in the documentation.
We added a cost note near the OpenAI mention in the documentation to clarify that embeddings are not expensive, costing ~$0.02 per million tokens.
The team removed any mention of Claude Code as a contributor from the README and package.json.
We decided to change the coverage measurement method on the landing page to include human-in-the-loop validation.
We decided to recommend Claude Sonnet 4.6 specifically for its features.
We chose a Claude Code Max subscription to provide sufficient token budget for most corpora.
We decided to recommend 24 GB RAM instead of 32 GB for Ollama.
We decided to make Knowledge Index sections clickable and expandable for better user interaction.
We will use the new component ClaimsExplorer.tsx to render a grouped searchable list of claims.
We decided to set all claim layer weights to 1.0 for both test_eval and strategic query modes.
We chose to add a new 'Retrieval' tab with slider inputs on the Settings page for adjusting observation, interpretation, and intention weights.
We will use user-defined layer weights from the retrieval_config column instead of hardcoded LAYER_WEIGHTS constant.
We will give beta testers access to a clean extraction from the private repo instead of the stale public one.
We decided to stop all development on the public repo until beta signal justifies the maintenance cost.
We decided to password-protect the docs site to reframe it as beta documentation for invited testers.
We decided to conduct a closed beta with 5-10 testers instead of an open-source launch.
We chose closed beta testing over open-source launch to gain insights from actual usage.
We accepted the cost of limited initial exposure to gain valuable feedback from a small group of testers.
We decided to ship brief and stress_test as two modes of a briefing-first evaluation system.
We ruled out buildBriefingPayload() sending fields like date, authority, claims, or source type distinction because the codebase audit identified these omissions.
We decided to keep the name 'brief' and enrich it with additional sections.
We created a new tool named 'scan' for Builder PMs to use frequently.
We decided to majorly rewrite 'stress_test' with new features and expanded verdict options.
We decided to prioritize ephemeral retrieval 'per user query' with minimal retention for Slack data ingestion due to API restrictions.
We are taking the hosted deployment path for Supabase per tenant to reduce beta setup time.
We decided to freeze the public repository because it has bugs and creates maintenance burden with no users.
We will provide beta testers access through a concierge setup instead of using the stale public repository.
The team decided on a similarity threshold of 0.95 for detecting structural changes in content.
The team accepted the risk of false positives to avoid missing material changes in content.
The team plans to adjust the threshold if stale claims are found or if false positives become costly at scale.
We chose Cursor over other tools because it is MCP-native and has a natural query moment for PMs.
We're sunsetting Slack bot in favor of Cursor/Claude Code due to higher reach and lower fidelity for judgment queries.
We ruled out Perplexity / ChatGPT because there is no MCP support today.
We decided to remove Claude Code from the Contributors section, listing only the founder.
We decided to adopt the 4-tool design from ILP v3 over the 11-tool surface of Assay.
We chose the brief output format of prompt+evidence from ILP v3 over the parsed JSON result of Assay.
We decided on per-tool sync for public distribution instead of using SyncManager from ILP v3.
The organization committed to behavioral success criteria for trust in PRD 3: 50% act on non-Safe verdicts and 70% expand citations.
We decided to split claims into 'Corpus Claims' and 'Evaluation Claims' using two queries.
We chose to use two separate RPC calls for 'Corpus Claims' and 'Evaluation Claims'.
We chose the Claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 model for claims extraction because it is hardcoded in the API route.
The team decided to update existing evidence records for tests with the same source reference.
The team chose to insert a new evidence record if no existing one is found for the given source reference.
We chose to set default layer weights if fetching from provider_settings fails.
We decided to capture whether the ILIKE fallback was used in metadata.
We decided to deactivate the current prompt and insert a new epistemology-based prompt.
We chose to use an epistemological framework for evaluating product proposals.
The team decided to filter claims by source prefix, specifically 'notion' or 'test'.
The team chose to filter claims by checking if 'parent_source_ref' starts with specific URLs for 'notion' and 'test'.
We decided to use Claude Haiku 4.5 for claim extraction due to its efficiency and lower cost.
We chose Claude Sonnet 4.6 for the evaluation step due to its superior reasoning capabilities.
We're using Claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 hardcoded in the current production route due to quality considerations at prototype scale.
We decided to integrate with cursor/copilot-style tools as the primary interface for our product.
We decided to ship the RETRIEVAL LOGIC (V1, no embeddings) for its strict default scope filter and text relevance.
We chose to seed 15–25 evidence records across at least 2 products and 3 projects.
We decided to update the Graph View to include source-linking control plane features.
We chose to add a lightweight version of the Integrations panel in V1.
We decided to add a compact default text area in the composer.
We decided to ship the LLM module for evaluating test proposals.
We decided to add an editable system operation prompt textarea and persist prompt versions.
We decided to ship the LLM Provider dropdown with only anthropic as an option.
We chose to save API keys in server-side storage and use them for runtime evaluation.
We ruled out exposing raw API keys to clients for security reasons.
We will wrap proposal insert, evaluation insert, and matches insert in one transaction for evaluate-and-save operations.
We decided to support inline modal creation for new products on /new-test.
We chose to treat proposal content as canonical fields including test_title, objective, prd_body, and additional_notes.
We ruled out using Project, Test Type, and Method fields in the LLM payload for evaluation input.
Remove direct SDK synthesis from `lib/briefingCore.ts` and route through the caller's Claude via MCP.
Delete `lib/anthropicInvoker.ts` and strip the Anthropic call from `maybeRunAnalyzer`, keeping pool-builder + renderer as pure functions.
Kill the SDK path immediately due to violation of product constraints.
The team decided to ship `briefing.analyzer.enabled` feature with default set to false.
Delete `lib/anthropicInvoker.ts`, strip the Anthropic call from `maybeRunAnalyzer`.
The team decided to ship the MVP of the memory work if it improves builder experience.
The team accepted a minimal additional effort for the memory subsystem if it aids beta performance.
The team decided to delete `lib/anthropicInvoker.ts` as part of the migration process.
Spec v1.1 updated to v1.2 to handle schema collisions for migration 026.
Migration runner adjusted to execute CREATE TABLE before CREATE INDEX for migration 026.
The team decided to map `high|medium|low` confidence levels in the existing schema to `high|moderate|low` as per the new specification.
We decided to ship the migration 026 decision primitive schema.
We decided to enable the flag for Assay Briefing Analyzer.
We decided to use phi4:14b model via Ollama for decision prompt backfill.
Added a post-filter to reject template-leak outputs and ensure evidence quotes exist in source content.
Cleaned contaminated rows from the database after killing the extraction process.
Approved using hardcoded Sonnet for extraction
Disable the Anthropic API on all web routes, using local Phi-4 extraction
Ruled out Perplexity / ChatGPT due to lack of MCP support
Sunsetting Slack bot in favor of Cursor/Claude Code
We decided to ship the SDK without requiring an API key.
We applied migration schema 026 and 027.
Rename 'claims' table to 'decision table'
Kill the SDK path immediately due to violation of product constraints
Remove direct SDK synthesis from `lib/briefingCore.ts` and route through caller's Claude via MCP
Compute decision content_hash as sha256(canonical|impact|sessionId|session_drain|decision) for ambient tag drain
Anchor session-drained decisions to a sentinel evidence record keyed by source_ref='session://<sessionId>' rather than reusing the first-enabled corpus record
Use structured child tags `<statement>` and `<reasoning>` instead of em-dash separator for the ambient decision tag format
Ship migration 029 with claims.decision_layer column plus an append-only decision_transitions audit table
Make assay_decision_expand walk the predecessor chain depth-N up to ten hops with cycle guard, returning supersedes_chain alongside the depth-1 supersedes pointer
Add includeSuperseded option to recallDecisions returning active matches plus a sidecar of recently-superseded decisions tagged with their replacement pointers
Replace em-dash separator decision with a full structured tag spec including supersedes attribute for explicit reversal-tracking
Test the resolver rejection path with a deliberately too-short 8-character prefix to confirm the v1.3 hardening actually fails closed
Pre-outreach gauntlet 2026-04-26: doctor 5/5, smokes 6/6, secrets scan clean, 210 decisions in ledger. Application certified ready for first-tester handoff.
Auto-supersession in lib/autoSupersession.ts uses cosine 0.78 floor + 3-token-minimum statement diff + 30-day lookback + same-impact-class gate, default audit-only without ASSAY_SUPERSEDE_FLIP=1.
T3 cross-session past_session tier in assay_recall cascade is opt-in via ASSAY_RECALL_INCLUDE_LEARNED=1, default OFF for trust-boundary reasons.

Cascade decision-tier cosine floor stays at 0.65 for v1 closed beta. The 40-query sweep showed 0.65 has 12% adjacent-class FPR but identical recall to higher floors; paraphrase top-1 accuracy of 53% i
Telemetry uses local JSONL at ~/.assay/telemetry.jsonl, gated by ASSAY_TELEMETRY=on, with sha256 query_hash by default and opt-in raw query via ASSAY_TELEMETRY_INCLUDE_QUERY=1. Concierge collects via

links.reason: Superseded by drained decision be4e2495: Replace em-dash separator decision with a full structured tag spec including supersedes attribute for explicit reversal-
